title: find_header_end searches CRLF before LF — prevents false split on LF
description:
find_header_end searches for 

 first and only falls back to 

. This ordering is correct: if the message uses CRLF line endings, a 

 search would produce a false match on the LF bytes inside the CRLF pairs. Searching CRLF-first ensures we split at the actual separator and not mid-sequence. The two-pass approach (search, then fallback) is simpler and correct given that real messages use one consistent line-ending style.
